title: polypodium pedicularis
intro: Polypodium pedicularis, also known as Polypodium leucotomos, is a type of fern species native to Central and South America. It is commonly referred to as "Mexican fire breast" due to its bright red underside of its fronds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and photoprotective effects. The health benefits of Polypodium pedicularis include protecting against skin damage from UV radiation, reducing oxidative stress, and potentially supporting the immune system, with herbal preparations often in the form of capsules, tablets, or topical creams.
